mnemonics: HADOOP: Handles All Data, Offers Outstanding Processing
keyPoints: Designed for distributed storage and processing of very large datasets across clusters of computers.
Provides a scalable and cost-effective solution compared to traditional relational database systems.
Its ecosystem of tools (like MapReduce, HDFS, Hive, Pig) allows for a variety of data processing tasks.
commonMistakes: Thinking Hadoop is a database; it is a framework for distributed storage and processing.
Believing it is for small datasets or real-time processing; it is optimized for batch processing large volumes of data.
Assuming it replaces all data storage needs; it is often part of a larger data architecture alongside other systems.
